# AbstractSkill

> The Agent Skills (`SKILL.md`) contract library for AbstractFramework: parse
> and validate skills, discover them on disk with progressive disclosure, hash
> them for evolution and tamper detection, compose their tool declarations with
> an operator grant, and classify their trust. Dependency-light (PyYAML only);
> executes nothing and imposes no runtime limits ‚Äî hosts consume its contracts.

## Document index

- README.md ‚Äî overview, package scope, hashing contract
- docs/getting-started.md ‚Äî parse, discover, hash, compose, evaluate trust
- docs/architecture.md ‚Äî components, data flow, verdict precedence
- docs/api.md ‚Äî public functions and types
- docs/faq.md ‚Äî common questions and limitations
- docs/troubleshooting.md ‚Äî symptoms, diagnostics, and fixes
- docs/trust.md ‚Äî trust model
- docs/trust-network-position.md ‚Äî join / leverage / build
- SECURITY.md ‚Äî guarantees and non-guarantees

---

## Overview

"Flows run; skills are activated." AbstractSkill owns the portable skill
contract so `abstractruntime`, `abstractgateway`, and thin clients share
identical semantics without duplicating parsers.

Install with `pip install abstractskill`. Python ‚â•3.10, PyYAML only.

Package surface:

- `parse_skill_md` ‚Äî YAML frontmatter + markdown body (LF/CRLF/CR;
  spec-validated name/description/compatibility).
- `FilesystemSkillLoader` ‚Äî metadata-only discovery and full-document loading;
  `discover()` and `load()` resolve identically (a broken copy never shadows a
  valid one) and degrade loudly (`#FALLBACK`).
- `content_hash` ‚Äî SHA-256 of one document.
- `hash_skill_tree` / `inspect_skill_dir` / `read_skill_resource` ‚Äî whole-tree
  tamper hash (injective manifest), structural inventory (`has_scripts` is a
  structural fact), bounded in-tree resource reads.
- `effective_tools` / `effective_tools_for_skill` ‚Äî grant ‚à© allowed-tools
  composition (narrows below the grant, never widens; absence implies nothing).
- `format_available_skills_xml` ‚Äî deterministic `<available_skills>` block.
- `evaluate_trust` + `TrustRegistry` / `ValidationRecord` / `AdvisoryEntry` /
  `GuidanceEntry` / `TrustVerdict` ‚Äî trust classification.
- `select_skills_for_context` ‚Üí `SkillSelection` ‚Äî the ONE trust-gated
  activation pipeline (load ‚Üí hash ‚Üí derive sources ‚Üí worst verdict ‚Üí gate);
  names-only calls derive provenance from the registry
  (`TrustRegistry.source_candidates_for`; advisories checked against EVERY
  candidate source so a losing record or wrong caller string cannot evade).
- `load_catalog`/`CatalogEntry`/`lint_catalog` ‚Äî the curated vendoring
  catalog contract (registry/catalog.yaml: reviewed entries pinned to 40-hex
  upstream commits + whole-tree hashes; owner/repo slugs only). The install
  path is CURATED-ONLY: `scripts/vendor_skill.py` fetches exactly the pinned
  commit, refuses symlinks/name-mismatches/byte drift, and validation records
  derive from the catalog (manual-review ‚Üí adopted; scripts-bearing skills
  stay requires_review). Curation list + tiers: docs/skills-catalog.md.

### Hashing contract: hash = bytes, parse = meaning

`content_hash` and `hash_skill_tree` are byte-exact deliberately ‚Äî tamper
detection must never call two byte-different trees "the same". A CRLF-authored
skill and its LF twin parse identically but hash differently: vendor skills
from archives or byte-copies, never through EOL-rewriting checkouts.

---

## Trust model (summary)

Trust binds to the tree hash, never the name ‚Äî any byte change voids a
validation. Levels: unverified < community < adopted (reviewed) < audited <
first_party; `blocked` is a verdict an advisory forces. A validation method
caps the level it can grant, so "reviewed" cannot masquerade as "audited".

A do-not-use `AdvisoryEntry` names a SPECIFIC skill with four mandated fields
(official_intent, hidden_issue, severity, reference). Severity is graded:
critical/high hard-block, hash-matched always blocks, low/medium force review.
`registry/advisories.yaml` is empty at v1 ‚Äî AbstractSkill does not self-assert
a specific malicious skill before its own audit or a leveraged feed names one.

A `GuidanceEntry` is a CATEGORY-level risk notice; it informs but never blocks
a specific skill. `registry/guidance.yaml` ships three notices grounded in
published 2026 research.

`evaluate_trust(...)` returns a fail-closed `TrustVerdict` with three
orthogonal signals: `blocked` (never attach), `requires_review` (operator
decides ‚Äî unverified, scripts present, or low/medium advisory), and
`attachable` (green path only: validated, advisory-free, script-free). Every
verdict lists the reasons it rests on.

Name-anchored advisories match on `(name, source)` pairs ‚Äî names
case-insensitively (spec-lowercase, normalized at construction and query
boundaries), sources exactly (stripped, case-sensitive; `lint_registry`
flags case-only near-misses and unknown sources at refresh time, warns
never refuses). Names-only context configs still match them:
`select_skills_for_context` derives candidate sources from validation records
(hash-bound = exact provenance, name-bound = prior-tree claim, warned) and
checks every candidate, keeping the worst verdict.

Trust classification raises the bar; it does not certify safety. Curation,
behavioral audit (backlog 0003), and the fail-closed default remain the real
gate.

---

## Trust-network position (summary)

A skill trust network is forming but immature (the agentskills.io signature
RFC, AgentGuard's attest/revoke registry, OWASP's Agentic Skills Top 10).
Position: BUILD now (the curated hash-pinned registry is the framework's trust
root), LEVERAGE next (consume external findings as advisory inputs with
provenance, human-merge), JOIN signing when the RFC stabilizes (the whole-tree
hash already gives integrity; a verified signature becomes evidence for a
validation). Federated signing proves authenticity, not safety, so it
corroborates the audit; it does not replace it.

---

For the full text of each document, read the linked files in the repository.
These indexes are discoverability aids, not canonical documentation.
